Function: Low-power BLE advertising tag with Open Braille Beacon ID/metadata
Key Specs: nRF52832 BLE module; 16 x 10 x 2.2 mm; 1.7–3.6 V supply; 512 KB Flash / 64 KB RAM; BLE + 2.4 GHz; RSSI; GPIO/SPI/UART/I2C/I2S/PWM/ADC/NFC.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Core part frozen. If Raytac is not available locally, use Digi-Key/Mouser/TME/Amazon import. Unit Electronics did not appear to list Raytac directly during search.
Electrical Notes: Use only 1x CR2032 or regulated 3 V. Do NOT use 2x CR2032 in series. Add 100 nF decoupling and 10–47 uF bulk. Respect SWD programming pads.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Keep module antenna at PCB edge. No copper, battery, screws, or metal under/above antenna keep-out.
Risk / Decision: Approved core tag module. Requires careful RF layout and sourcing verification.
Approved Alternatives: MDBT42Q-P512KV2, other nRF52832/nRF52 BLE modules with similar voltage/current/radio layout requirements.

Function: Primary battery for silent BLE tag
Key Specs: 3 V nominal, 20 mm diameter, ~3.2 mm thick; typical capacity ~200 mAh depending brand/load.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Widely available in Mexico: electronics shops, supermarkets, Amazon/MercadoLibre, Steren/Unit-type stores.
Electrical Notes: Coin cells dislike high current peaks. BLE advertising must be low duty cycle. Bulk capacitor recommended near module.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Leave access for battery replacement. Battery cavity ~21 mm diameter if direct, or use exact holder footprint.
Risk / Decision: Approved. Battery life must be measured on bench, not guessed.
Approved Alternatives: Renata, Panasonic, Energizer, Maxell CR2032 from reputable source.

Recommended Part: Single CR2032 PCB holder, e.g. HandsOn CON1118 or Keystone-style single holder
Key Specs: Single-cell holder for 20 mm coin cells; example dimensions 24.3 x 7.4 mm; PCB mount; PBT body.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Common as 'porta pila CR2032 para PCB' in Unit/Sterén/MercadoLibre/Amazon; verify exact footprint before PCB.
Electrical Notes: Must be single-cell 3 V. Avoid dual CR2032 series holders unless adding a regulator, which is not recommended for tag v1.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Model exact holder before final CAD. Placeholder: ~24 x 22 x 5 mm if horizontal holder is used.
Risk / Decision: Approved only as single-cell holder. Blueprint dual-cell holder rejected.
Approved Alternatives: Keystone 1060/1061/1058 style, any single CR2032 holder with known footprint and low profile.

Recommended Part: Littelfuse MRPR-3 miniature normally-open reed switch
Function: Magnetic setup/reset trigger without exposed public button
Key Specs: SPST-NO; glass envelope 19.69 x 2.84 mm; 22–43 AT; zero operating power; contact resistance <100 mΩ; -40 to +125 °C.
Availability / Mexico Notes: If exact MRPR-3 is hard to source, use generic NO reed switch locally. Verify size and activation distance with chosen magnet.
Electrical Notes: Use with internal pull-up or 100 kΩ external pull-up to reduce current. Debounce in firmware.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Place close to side wall, ~1–1.5 mm from exterior. Add external tactile/magnet mark.
Risk / Decision: Approved concept. Exact sensitivity must be bench-tested with actual magnet and enclosure plastic thickness.
Approved Alternatives: Any miniature SPST-NO reed switch with known AT range and manageable size.

Key Specs: Any low-current visible LED; choose green or amber for visibility; forward voltage depends color.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Very common locally. Use whatever footprint is easiest to solder in prototype.
Electrical Notes: Use resistor 1 kΩ–4.7 kΩ. LED must be normally off. Avoid constant blinking in production.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Align with 4.0 x 1.5 mm window or light pipe. Keep window filleted.
Risk / Decision: Approved, but only if firmware keeps it mostly off to preserve battery.
Approved Alternatives: 0603/0805 red, green, amber LED from any common brand.

Function: Local high-frequency decoupling for BLE module
Key Specs: 0.1 uF; ceramic; rated >=6.3 V; X7R/X5R preferred.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Common locally. Buy assorted SMD capacitor kit if needed.
Electrical Notes: Place close to VDD/GND pins of module. Short traces.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: No major CAD impact; reserve PCB space near module.
Approved Alternatives: Any reputable 100 nF ceramic capacitor in selected footprint.

Function: Buffers BLE radio current peaks from CR2032
Key Specs: 10–47 uF, >=6.3 V, low ESR preferred. Actual capacitance derates with DC bias.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Common, but check package and capacitance availability.
Electrical Notes: Place close to module power rail. Helps avoid voltage dip during advertising bursts.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Use low-profile part. Avoid interfering with top cover.
Approved Alternatives: 10 uF + 100 nF minimum; 47 uF if space and leakage are acceptable.

Key Specs: Expose VDD, GND, SWDIO, SWDCLK, RESET if possible.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Pads cost nothing. Tag-Connect cable optional/import. Pogo pads can be DIY for early prototypes.
Electrical Notes: Do not omit. Required before sealed enclosure/field use.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Make accessible before final assembly or through temporary opening/test jig.
Risk / Decision: Mandatory. Lack of programming pads is a PCB-killer.
Approved Alternatives: 2.54 mm header pads for prototype; SWD pogo pad layout for final.

Function: BLE scanning receiver, one-button UX, haptic control
Key Specs: Nordic nRF52840; Bluetooth 5.0; 21 x 17.5 mm; USB-C; LiPo charge management; 1 MB flash / 256 KB RAM; 11 digital I/O; 6 analog I/O; 3.3 V I/O only; ~5 uA deep sleep.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Listed by Unit Electronics search results as XIAO nRF52840 BLE. Also available via Seeed, Digi-Key, Amazon/MercadoLibre imports.
Electrical Notes: All I/O are 3.3 V only. Board already includes USB-C and LiPo charger, useful for receiver prototype.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Model 21 x 17.5 mm board with USB-C access and antenna keep-out. Do not enclose antenna with metal.
Risk / Decision: Selected for receiver prototype, not necessarily final custom PCB.
Approved Alternatives: nRF52840 Pro Micro, Adafruit Feather nRF52840, Nordic nRF52840 DK for bench only.

Key Specs: Example 150 mAh: 3.7 V nominal; charge voltage 4.2 V; recommended charge current 0.2C; max charge 0.5C; output 4.2–2.5 V; protection available; ~15 x 12 x 9 mm for one 150 mAh model.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Common locally as LiPo 3.7 V with JST-PH. Verify connector polarity before plugging into XIAO/charger.
Electrical Notes: Use protected cell or protection PCB. Never charge unattended during early tests. Match charger current to battery capacity.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Leave swelling clearance. Do not crush, screw through, or overbend pouch cell.
Approved Alternatives: 200 mAh or 300 mAh protected LiPo if enclosure allows.

Priority: Only if not using XIAO charger
Key Specs: Linear charger; CC/CV algorithm; 4.20/4.35/4.40/4.50 V options; programmable charge current 15–500 mA; charge status output; thermal regulation; SOT-23-5/DFN.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Common on LiPo charger modules and through Digi-Key/Mouser. Not needed if XIAO charger is used directly.
Electrical Notes: Set charge current appropriately. Add input/output caps per datasheet. Charger alone is not full battery protection unless cell has PCM.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Thermal relief and USB-C position matter in custom PCB.
Risk / Decision: Deferred for custom receiver PCB. XIAO handles charging in prototype.
Approved Alternatives: TP4056 module for bench/prototype only; BQ25101/BQ25100 family for compact final design.

Recommended Part: Vybronics VC1030B028F or generic 10 mm x 3 mm 3 V coin motor
Key Specs: 10 mm diameter; 3 mm thick; 3.0 V rated; 2.7–3.3 V operating range; 64 mA typical, 85 mA max; 13,500 rpm; 0.90 Grms; adhesive pad.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Generic 3 V coin motors widely available in robotics/electronics stores, Amazon/MercadoLibre.
Electrical Notes: Never drive directly from GPIO. Use MOSFET/driver and consider flyback/transient protection. PWM can control intensity.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Motor must be mechanically coupled to enclosure wall. Adhesive mount helps transfer vibration.
Risk / Decision: Selected. Bench-test feel through clip enclosure.
Approved Alternatives: Any 3 V coin vibration motor around 8–10 mm with current <100 mA.

Key Specs: 30 V VDS; 5.8 A at VGS=10 V; RDS(on) <52 mΩ at VGS=2.5 V; gate threshold 0.65–1.45 V; SOT-23.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Very common as AO3400/AO3400A MOSFET module or SMD part. Unit/Santayc/Amazon/MercadoLibre likely carry equivalents.
Electrical Notes: Use gate resistor ~100 Ω and pulldown ~100 kΩ. Source to GND, drain to motor negative, motor positive to battery/3.3 V rail as designed.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Small SOT-23 footprint. Keep near motor connector/pads.
Risk / Decision: Selected. Overkill but cheap and safe for motor current.
Approved Alternatives: IRLML2502, DMG2302, 2N7002 only if motor current is low enough.

Function: Transient/freewheel protection for motor or polarity protection if used
Key Specs: Schottky rectifier; 40 V reverse; 1 A average forward current; low forward voltage; fast switching.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Extremely common locally. SMD equivalent SS14 also common.
Electrical Notes: For a brushed motor, place diode across motor if needed. Some coin motor setups use TVS/diode/snubber depending noise.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: DO-41 through-hole is large; prefer SMD SS14/SOD-123 for compact PCB.
Risk / Decision: Recommended. Choose SMD package for clip compactness.
Approved Alternatives: SS14, BAT54 for small-signal, or TVS/snubber if EMI becomes a problem.

Recommended Part: Würth 450302014072 or generic mini SPDT slide switch
Key Specs: SPDT on-on; 300 mA @ 24 VDC; contact resistance 30 mΩ max initial; -40 to +85 °C; 10 x 2.5 mm right-angle example.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Generic mini slide switches widely available. Exact Würth may require Digi-Key/Mouser.
Electrical Notes: Switch battery or enable line depending board. Verify current rating with motor peak.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Needs side access in clip enclosure. Protect from accidental toggles but keep reachable.
Risk / Decision: Selected for receiver. Tag does not need public slide switch.
Approved Alternatives: Any SPDT/SPST mini slide switch rated >=300 mA at low voltage.

Recommended Part: 6 x 6 mm tactile switch, e.g. C&K PTS645 or generic 6x6x5 mm
Function: One-button UX: short press cycles tags, long press selects/cancels
Key Specs: SPST normally-open momentary; 50 mA @ 12 VDC; compact 6 x 6 mm; C&K PTS645 life expectancy ~100,000 operations.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Generic 6x6 tact switches are everywhere; choose height based on enclosure.
Electrical Notes: Use internal pull-up/pull-down. Debounce in firmware. Add ESD protection if exposed heavily.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Button must be findable by touch. Consider raised external button cap.
Risk / Decision: Selected. UX reliability depends on button feel and firmware debounce.
Approved Alternatives: Any tactile switch with comfortable force and known height.

Recommended Part: 3M VHB double-sided foam tape, thin variant
Key Specs: High-bond double-sided adhesive foam tape; select thickness ~0.8–1.2 mm if possible.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Available in hardware/electronics stores and MercadoLibre/Amazon.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Backplate should include 30 x 25 x 0.3 mm recessed zone to position adhesive.
Risk / Decision: Recommended with optional screws for public installation robustness.
Approved Alternatives: Screws only, VHB + screws, removable backplate.

Recommended Part: M2 for enclosure closure; M3 optional for wall mounting
Function: Mechanical closure and optional wall mount
Key Specs: M2 screws for small plastic bosses; M3 optional holes for surface mounting.
Availability / Mexico Notes: Screw kits available on Amazon/MercadoLibre/local hardware stores.
Electrical Notes: Do not place metal screws near BLE antenna keep-out.
CAD / Mechanical Notes: Boss OD ~4.0–4.8 mm, pilot hole ~1.6–1.8 mm for M2. Add base fillets.
Approved Alternatives: Heat-set inserts later; snap-fit only in v2.
